mnemonics: Powers Predict Paths: Raising a stochastic matrix to the nth power gives the probabilities of transitioning from state i to state j in exactly n steps.
keyPoints: A stochastic matrix is a square matrix where each row sums to 1, representing probabilities.
The entries P_ij represent the probability of moving FROM state i TO state j.
The long-term behavior of a system can be analyzed by finding the steady-state vector, which satisfies πP = π.
commonMistakes: Assuming the matrix is symmetric; rows sum to 1, but columns generally do not.
Confusing the direction of the transition (e.g., thinking P_ij is probability from j to i instead of i to j).
Forgetting that the powers of the matrix represent multi-step transitions.
